steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows the sentence introducing the 899 BCE eclipse as 'annular.' The next sentence explains what 'annular' means: the Moon didn't cover the Sun completely, creating a ring of fire. The transition must signal that the second sentence defines or restates the term introduced in the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: 'For example' introduces a specific instance of a general claim. The second sentence isn't an example of annularity — it's the definition of it. ✗
  • Choice B — Meanwhile,: 'Meanwhile' signals something happening at the same time. Nothing in the passage is simultaneous — this is a clarification, not a parallel event. ✗
  • Choice C — That is,: 'That is' signals a restatement or definition — exactly what the second sentence does by explaining what 'annular' means. ✓
  • Choice D — Nonetheless,: 'Nonetheless' signals contrast despite an obstacle. The second sentence doesn't contrast with the first — it clarifies it. ✗
That matches Choice C — That is,.
